title: murraya glabra
intro: Murraya glabra, also known as the yellow jessamine or satinwood, is a flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Murraya glabra include reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
